description: As a Systems Administrator monitoring a Linux fleet via Splunk (http://localhost:8000, credentials: admin / SplunkAdmin1!), configure an observability dashboard for critical kernel events. Create three event types in the Search & Reporting app named 'linux_oom_event' (searching index=system_logs for "Out of memory" OR "oom-killer"), 'linux_hardware_fault' (searching index=system_logs for "Machine Check Exception" OR "Hardware Error"), and 'linux_disk_error' (searching index=system_logs for "I/O error" OR "EXT4-fs error"). Apply the tag 'system_failure' to these event types and construct a Classic Dashboard named 'Linux_Kernel_Health' containing at least two visualization panels that utilize the 'system_failure' tag in their SPL searches.
